stimulus: "All that is gold does not glitter, not all those who wander are lost." This line is from J. R. R. Tolkien's novel The Fellowship of the Ring (1954). It's an example of chiasmus, a literary technique that ______blank contrast by reversing the structure of two related phrases.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-verb agreement. The subject of the relative clause is the singular relative pronoun "that," referring to the singular noun "technique." A singular verb ("creates") is therefore required.

Choice A is incorrect because the plural progressive "are creating" doesn't agree with the singular antecedent. Choice B is incorrect because the plural "have created" doesn't agree with the singular antecedent. Choice C is incorrect because the plural "create" doesn't agree with the singular antecedent.
